subject: skl_content-strategy-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines3-980529e8-f920698-skills-content-strategy-skill-md_980529e8
evidence: content-strategy analysis: 'Each source gets a specific extraction method: extract questions asked → FAQ content, extract pain points → problems in their own words'. copywriting analysis: 'The context-gathering section asks the right questions but doesn't help when answers are thin. It assumes someone — the user or a product-marketing context file — knows who the customer is, what they care about, and what language they use. Without rich voice-of-customer input, the copy will be generic regardless of skill quality.' → chains_with, weight 0.82. Content-strategy extracts customer language, pain points, and competitive positioning from research inputs; copywriting consumes those exact inputs as its brief to produce non-generic web page copy. Content-strategy produces the research foundation that copywriting requires to avoid its primary failure mode of generic output.

subject: skl_copywriting-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines31-mar-42797825-nes-ef920698-skills-copywriting-skill-md_42797825
evidence: copywriting analysis: 'There's no treatment of how copy performs when read by AI summarizers, snippet extractors, or voice assistants. A sibling skill (ai-seo) exists for that, but the assumption that web copy is read by humans is increasingly incomplete.' ai-seo analysis: 'This skill teaches agents how to optimize content so AI systems — ChatGPT, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews, Claude, Gemini, Copilot — cite it in their answers.' 'The skill walks them through an audit template, then prescribes concrete structural and authority changes with expected visibility boosts per method.' → chains_with, weight 0.82. Copywriting produces human-readable persuasive web copy; ai-seo takes that copy as input and applies structural patterns (answer blocks, comparison tables, llms.txt creation) to make it extractable and citable by LLMs. The copywriting analysis explicitly names ai-seo as the skill that handles AI-consumed copy.

subject: skl_cro-src-https-github-com-coreyhaines31-marketingsk-e9567f9f-orey-haines-ef920698-skills-cro-skill-md_e9567f9f
evidence: CRO analysis: 'The primary risk is producing advice that sounds specific but isn't. Add social proof near the CTA is actionable enough to implement but not specific enough to work — add what social proof? The skill provides the what-to-check framework but not the how-to-decide.' Copywriting analysis: 'The page-structure framework (headline → social proof → problem → solution → how it works → objections → final CTA) is standard persuasion architecture.' 'The output format ensures the human gets not just copy but reasoning for every choice plus alternatives.' → strengthens, weight 0.75. CRO identifies which page sections are underperforming (missing social proof, weak headlines, poor CTAs) but lacks the specificity to fix them; copywriting provides the structured section-by-section templates and persuasion architecture to rewrite those sections with concrete, implementable copy. CRO's 'Copy Alternatives' output category is fulfilled by copywriting's annotated copy generation.
